perturbation.original_text: Valeant shall follow all instructions and guidelines of Dova (of which Dova has provided Valeant copies) in connection with the use of any Dova Trademarks and Copyrights, and, if Dova reasonably objects to the manner in which any such Dova Trademarks and Copyrights are being used, Valeant shall cease the use of any such Dova Trademarks and Copyrights in such manner upon written notice from Dova thereof. Without limiting the foregoing, Valeant shall also adhere to at least the same quality control provisions as companies in the pharmaceutical industry adhere to for their own trademarks and copyrights. In all cases, Valeant shall use the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights with the necessary trademark (and copyright, as applicable) designations, and shall use the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights in a manner that does not derogate from Dova's rights in the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights. Valeant shall not at any time during the Term knowingly do or allow to be done any act or thing which will in any way impair or diminish the rights of Dova in or to the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights. All goodwill and
perturbation.changed_text: Valeant shall follow all instructions and guidelines of Dova (of which Dova has provided Valeant copies) in connection with the use of any Dova Trademarks and Copyrights, and, if Dova reasonably objects to the manner in which any such Dova Trademarks and Copyrights are being used, Valeant shall cease the use of any such Dova Trademarks and Copyrights in such manner upon written notice from Dova thereof. Without limiting the foregoing, Valeant shall also adhere to at least the same quality control provisions as companies in the pharmaceutical industry adhere to for their own trademarks and copyrights. In all cases, Valeant shall use the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights with or without the necessary trademark (and copyright, as applicable) designations, and may use the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights in a manner that does not derogate from Dova's rights in the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights unless otherwise agreed by both parties in writing. Valeant may at any time during the Term knowingly do or allow to be done any act or thing which will in any way impair or diminish the rights of Dova in or to the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights, given an approval from DOVA. All goodwill and
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces a contradiction by stating that Valeant can use Dova's trademarks with or without necessary designations, and potentially in a way that could harm Dova's rights, with DOVA given a specific approval, this contradicts the original clause which requires Valeant to always use trademarks correctly and protect Dova's rights. This creates uncertainty about the permitted use of trademarks.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Valeant shall follow all instructions and guidelines of Dova (of which Dova has provided Valeant copies) in connection with the use of any Dova Trademarks and Copyrights, and, if Dova reasonably objects to the manner in which any such Dova Trademarks and Copyrights are being used, Valeant shall cease the use of any such Dova Trademarks and Copyrights in such manner upon written notice from Dova thereof. Without limiting the foregoing, Valeant shall also adhere to at least the same quality control provisions as companies in the pharmaceutical industry adhere to for their own trademarks and copyrights. In all cases, Valeant shall use the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights with or without the necessary trademark (and copyright, as applicable) designations, and may use the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights in a manner that does not derogate from Dova's rights in the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights unless otherwise agreed by both parties in writing. Valeant may at any time during the Term knowingly do or allow to be done any act or thing which will in any way impair or diminish the rights of Dova in or to the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights, given an approval from DOVA. All goodwill and improved reputation generated by Valeant's use of the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights shall inure to the benefit of Dova, and any use of the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights by Valeant shall cease at the end of the Term. Valeant shall have no rights under this Agreement in or to the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights except as specifically provided herein. During the Term, Valeant will not contest the ownership of the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights, their validity, or the validity of any registration therefor. During the Term, Valeant will not knowingly register and/or use any marks (including in connection with any domain names) that are confusingly similar to the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces contradictory statements. The original text states "Valeant shall use the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights with the necessary trademark (and copyright, as applicable) designations, and shall use the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights in a manner that does not derogate from Dova's rights in the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights. Valeant shall not at any time during the Term knowingly do or allow to be done any act or thing which will in any way impair or diminish the rights of Dova in or to the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights." The changed version says "Valeant shall use the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights with or without the necessary trademark (and copyright, as applicable) designations, and may use the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights in a manner that does not derogate from Dova's rights in the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights unless otherwise agreed by both parties in writing. Valeant may at any time during the Term knowingly do or allow to be done any act or thing which will in any way impair or diminish the rights of Dova in or to the Dova Trademarks and Copyrights, given an approval from DOVA." This directly contradicts the original requirement for Valeant to always use the trademarks correctly and protect Dova's rights, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Meetings of the JSC may be called by either Party on no less than thirty (30) days' notice during the Term. Each Party shall make all proposals for agenda items and shall provide all appropriate information with respect to such proposed items at least ten (10) days in advance to the applicable meeting; provided that under exigent circumstances requiring input by the JSC, a Party may provide its agenda items to the other Party within a shorter period of time in advance of the meeting, or may propose that there not be a specific agenda for that particular meeting, so long as the other Party consents to such later addition of such agenda items or the absence of a specific agenda for such meeting, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: Meetings of the JSC may be called by either Party on no less than fifteen (15) days' notice during the Term, or shorter if required. Each Party shall make all proposals for agenda items and shall provide all appropriate information with respect to such proposed items at least ten (10) days in advance to the applicable meeting; provided that under exigent circumstances requiring input by the JSC, a Party may provide its agenda items to the other Party within a shorter period of time in advance of the meeting, or may propose that there not be a specific agenda for that particular meeting, even if the other Party does not consents to such later addition of such agenda items or the absence of a specific agenda for such meeting.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the notice period for meetings to 'no less than fifteen (15) days or shorter if required' and removing the need for the other party's consent for agenda changes, the amendment directly contradicts the initial requirement for 30-day notice and mutual agreement, creating an in-text conflict. This introduces ambiguity in how meetings are convened and how agenda items are set, impacting the JSC's operational effectiveness.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Meetings of the JSC may be called by either Party on no less than fifteen (15) days' notice during the Term, or shorter if required. Each Party shall make all proposals for agenda items and shall provide all appropriate information with respect to such proposed items at least ten (10) days in advance to the applicable meeting; provided that under exigent circumstances requiring input by the JSC, a Party may provide its agenda items to the other Party within a shorter period of time in advance of the meeting, or may propose that there not be a specific agenda for that particular meeting, even if the other Party does not consents to such later addition of such agenda items or the absence of a specific agenda for such meeting. The chairperson shall prepare and circulate for review and approval of the Parties minutes of each meeting within thirty (30) days after the meeting. Each Party shall bear its own costs for its members to attend such meetings.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period for JSC meetings from 'no less than thirty (30) days' to 'no less than fifteen (15) days or shorter if required' and removes the requirement for the other party's consent for agenda changes. This directly contradicts the original intent and creates inconsistency regarding meeting scheduling and agenda setting, leading to a clear in-text contradiction as per the explanation. Because the changed text and contradicted text is the same, this signifies that the original text was modified to contradict itself. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines or obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the terms of this Section 4.2.1, Valeant shall have the right, from time to time, during the Term, to include in the incentive compensation package of all or some of the Sales Representatives a spiff, spiv or other similar incentive bonus that is based on [***], provided that the actual, maximum payout from such incentive bonuses does not exceed, in the aggregate, an amount equal to [***] for each Sales Representative for each Calendar Quarter. Any such spiff, spiv or other similar incentive bonus shall not be included in the calculation of the applicable Sales Representatives incentive compensation package in determining Valeant's compliance with the terms of Section 4.1.3.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the terms of this Section 4.2.1, Valeant shall have the right, from time to time, during the Term, to include in the incentive compensation package of all or some of the Sales Representatives a spiff, spiv or other similar incentive bonus that is based on [***], even if the actual, maximum payout from such incentive bonuses exceeds, in the aggregate, an amount equal to [***] for each Sales Representative for each Calendar Quarter. Any such spiff, spiv or other similar incentive bonus will be included in the calculation of the applicable Sales Representatives incentive compensation package in determining Valeant's compliance with the terms of Section 4.1.3.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying this section, allowing Valeant to exceed the maximum payout for incentive bonuses and including these bonuses in the calculation of compliance with Section 4.1.3, the modified term directly contradicts the limitations and exclusions established in the original. This in-text contradiction creates confusion about how incentive bonuses should be treated, affecting Valeant's compliance and potentially impacting the promotion fee calculation under Section 6.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the terms of this Section 4.2.1, Valeant shall have the right, from time to time, during the Term, to include in the incentive compensation package of all or some of the Sales Representatives a spiff, spiv or other similar incentive bonus that is based on [***], even if the actual, maximum payout from such incentive bonuses exceeds, in the aggregate, an amount equal to [***] for each Sales Representative for each Calendar Quarter. Any such spiff, spiv or other similar incentive bonus will be included in the calculation of the applicable Sales Representatives incentive compensation package in determining Valeant's compliance with the terms of Section 4.1.3.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement by allowing incentive bonuses to exceed the previously set maximum payout and including them in the calculation of Valeant's compliance with Section 4.1.3. The original text explicitly excluded these bonuses from compliance calculations and set a maximum payout. This creates a clear inconsistency and affects Valeant's compliance and promotion fee calculation.
